subject: President Trump’s message on last night’s midterms
body:
Midterm elections are usually tough going for whichever party controls the White House. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c5441bfeab&e=3ee4985eea)

| The White House • November 7, 2018
---
| | |
# President Trump’s message on last night’s midterms

**Midterm elections** are usually tough going for whichever party controls the White House. Over the previous 21 midterms, the president’s party has lost an average of four seats in the Senate. Only four times in that span has the president’s party gained seats in Congress’ upper chamber.

**Last night, President Donald J. Trump** and the Republican Party defied that history and expanded their Senate majority. Here are three key takeaways:
* The President’s message resonates across the heartland. He held 30 rallies in the past 60 days, and “of the 11 candidates we campaigned with during the last week, nine won last night,” President Trump said today.

* Across the country, in both Senate and House races, candidates who excelled embraced conservative messages of low taxes, low regulations, low crime, strong borders, and great judges. Candidates who struggled—including some Republicans—tried to distance themselves from these themes.

* America’s economy is booming, and voters in working-class communities are taking notice. Record job growth, historically low unemployment, the return of American manufacturing, and renegotiated trade deals earned Republicans strong support everywhere from Florida to Ohio to North Dakota.

**What’s next?** President Trump made his priority clear in a press conference from the White House today. “Now is the time for members of both parties to join together, put partisanship aside, and keep the American economic miracle going strong,” he said, citing infrastructure, trade, and lowering the cost of prescription drugs as three areas he believes Democrats may want to work with Republicans.

[Watch President Trump’s statement on last night’s midterm results](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=42a47868b3&e=3ee4985eea)

**More** : [The President says put the American people first, not partisan politics](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d5c89a965d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## President Trump and the free press

**President Trump’s unprecedented media access** reveals just how important transparency is to this Administration’s success.

**During today’s press conference** , President Trump took 68 questions—from 35 different reporters. By contrast, former President Obama took 22 questions from 10 reporters during his press conference following the 2010 midterm elections.

**CBS’ Major Garrett** put it crisply: “There is more access to this president than Obama . . . We see him and interact with him and punch in questions with far more frequency.” ABC News Political Director Rick Klein agrees, saying President Trump is “above and beyond, far and away more accessible” than our previous two presidents.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: What happened last night
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b7b8/2f5ea88d/7ba5d638/2cbd81a9/2899076079/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-next-win?utm_source=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&refcode=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&utm_campaign=day-after-election&utm_medium=ema") Friend, What a night. You and millions of Americans in our movement voted to elect seven Democratic challengers to governorships from coast to coast last night. This is the most seats that have changed hands in 24 years! In fact, the majority of Americans now live in states led by a Democratic governor. As chair of the Democratic Governors Association, I couldn't be more proud of the candidates, their staff, and volunteers that took on that fight. And now we're getting to work to advance our states, take on climate change, and build a clean energy future for our communities. Progressives across the country banded together to reject Donald Trump's hateful rhetoric and seized the majority in the U.S. House of Representatives. Right here in Washington state, we helped flip the House with an incredible win. We secured a more progressive state legislature. And we passed new, aggressive, and common-sense gun safety reforms. Last night was a huge victory not just for Democrats in the House, but for the future of America. Yes, we had some losses too. But I'm so proud of the collective work we did to win last night. [**But now, it's time to keep winning. Are you ready for the next big fight? Chip in now, and let's take this to the next level. We're building a progressive movement to last -- not just this cycle, but for generations to come.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b7b8/2f5ea88d/7ba5d638/2cbd81a9/2899076079/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-next-win?utm_source=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&refcode=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&utm_campaign=day-after-election&utm_medium=ema") Friend, we didn't just win big in Washington, D.C. last night -- we won big here in Washington state. With majorities in the state house and state senate, we'll continue to lead the country in our progressive policy initiatives. We are ranked first in the nation in our state economy, and we're ready to continue that legacy in education reform and clean energy policies. **This is what we set out to do. And this victory belongs to YOU.** But we need to keep working. We can't rest on our laurels. We've got to make sure our Washington legislature is ready to pass the most progressive climate legislation in the country when they resume their session in a few weeks. We've got to keep the pressure on the House to make sure they investigate Trump's ties with Russia. And we've got to keep winning in local, state, and national elections. It's the only way we'll defeat Trump and his enablers. So today, keep in mind that this fight continues. [**And to win, we need you by our side. Let's start early this time and build on the progress we've made -- we must keep winning. Are you ready? Donate now for our future victories together.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b7b8/2f5ea88d/7ba5d638/2cbd81a9/2899076079/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-next-win?utm_source=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&refcode=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&utm_campaign=day-after-election&utm_medium=ema") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b7b8/2f5ea88d/7ba5d638/2cbd81a9/2899076079/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-next-win?utm_source=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&refcode=ema_2018-11-07_day-after-election&utm_campaign=day-after-election&utm_medium=ema")

And this victory belongs to YOU. But we need to keep working. We can't rest on our laurels. We've got to make sure our Washington legislature is ready to pass the most progressive climate legislation in the country when they resume their session in a few weeks. We've got to keep the pressure on the House to make sure they investigate Trump's ties with Russia. And we've got to keep winning in local, state, and national elections. It's the only way we'll defeat Trump and his enablers. So today, keep in mind that this fight continues. "" Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | "" Jay Inslee is leading a movement to create long-lasting progressive change in America. Now more than ever, we must support progressive leadership in America. Chip in to show you're with Jay Inslee as we fight for progress in Washington state and around the country. | ""

body:

First, thank you so very much for your support in this race. I'm so grateful for the opportunity to continue to represent you in Washington.

It was a late night last night watching all the election results coming in from around the state and across the nation. So I'll keep this week's blog short. Click the link below to read my thoughts on the night's results. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-h/)
### [A Ceasefire, Quickly Broken](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-k/)
I just got home from a rally at a place in downtown Cincinnati on 6th Street, called The Backstage Event Center. Among the Republican candidates who spoke at this Boisterous event, one week before a major election, were Congressman Warren Davis, Senator Rob Portman, Ohio Attorney General Mike DeWine, and oh yeah, me. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-u/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-o/)
### [Two Weeks Out - So How's the Election Looking?](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-b/)
A lot better than it looked two months ago, or two weeks ago for that matter. I think things are trending in the right direction. The Democrats' advantage on the generic ballot has narrowed significantly - from double digits to low single digits. And in the so-called battleground congressional districts, the ones that are hotly contested this time around (like mine), and will decide which party controls the House after the election, the generic ballot is essentially a dead heat. ### [Trump, Robert E. Lee, Some Nut Hates Republicans, and Our First Debate Last Night](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtlyijkt-kujkulhluu-x/)
As I'm sure you know, President Trump came to town last Friday. And since I kind of had a ring side seat as the whole thing unfolded, I thought I'd share what I saw. Hi Peter,

**Thank you to all of you for supporting our campaign and giving me the honor of representing Illinois’ Third District for another two years in the U.S. House of Representatives.**

These results demonstrated that voters want more of my commonsense leadership that brings people together and has produced results for the district and the country.

Over the years I’ve authored dozens of laws which have resulted in cleaner air and water, increased protections from sexual assault, better roads and public transit, good-paying middle-class jobs, and other improvements for our region and the nation. Moving forward, I will continue to be a leader on these and other issues that are critical to making life better and giving people greater opportunities to live the American Dream no matter who they are or where they live.

This was a particularly hard-fought election across our nation because people are deeply concerned about where our country is going.

**Americans are sick and tired of the hateful rhetoric and fear-mongering.** We need a change in rhetoric and the change must start at the top, because all leaders and those who want to lead have a responsibility to treat every person with dignity.

I am humbled by your votes and honored to work for another two years in our Congress to extend that dignity to all Americans.

Let’s get to work,

Dan

name: Jason Yates
subject: God Is Not Surprised by the Election Results
body:
| | “Duty is ours, results are God’s.” -John Quincy Adams
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iZwN3saXAy-Y-UHwwC213Xlnn4bVFc-6IerhL0u1MQRCaHu99Dy2z7aKMEVT0HgHJLotWYSLvnj2G6pwgxnLlCU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181108com01&n=1)
| A politically active believer, John Quincy Adams, knew what it was like to win and lose. When asked why he continued to fight through opposition and even failure, Adams never wavered: “Duty is ours, results are God’s.” Tuesday, millions of Christians prayed and voted. Instead of sitting on the sidelines, Christians stepped up and took action, casting our votes for biblical values.

Of the more than 70,000 races nationwide—from local school board and municipal to state legislature, governorship, and judicial to U.S. House and Senate—chances are, not all went the way you voted. **But the results were not our responsibility. Our responsibility was seizing the opportunity to vote.** Galatians 6:10 is one of my favorite, impactful verses: “So then, as we have opportunity, let us do good to everyone, and especially to those who are of the household of faith” (ESV). While the context of the verse isn’t addressing voting per se, the apostle Paul’s instruction encompasses every opportunity we have as believers. Thankfully, in America, voting is our ability to take action and fight to bring good—the good of our faith—into our communities and our nation, as an example to the world. Luke 12:48 says: “...Everyone to whom much was given, of him much will be required, and from him to whom they entrusted much, they will demand the more” (ESV). Our good God gave us the opportunity to be faithful in these midterms—to activate our faith and bring about His good into our communities and our country. We were called to be faithful stewards—and leave the results up to Him. Scripture is clear: “Let every person be subject to the governing authorities. For there is no authority except from God, and those that exist have been instituted by God” (Romans 13:1 ESV). **What reassurance and peace to know we serve a sovereign God who is in complete control.** I want to encourage you to pray for your new leaders—regardless if they were the ones you voted for, or not. They will be on your school board, in your state legislature, your courts, and representing you in Washington, D.C. Pray for them right now, even before they take their oath of office in the New Year. Can you name your new leaders? To effectively pray for them and the important issues they will impact, we must know them by name. “First of all, then, I urge that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all people, for kings and all who are in high positions, that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life, godly and dignified in every way. This is good, and it is pleasing in the sight of God our Savior...” (1 Timothy 2:1-4 ESV). Praying for our leaders is essential to leading “a peaceful and quiet life, godly and dignified in every way.” **At My Faith Votes, we will continue to pray. But we won’t stop there.** We will bring the solutions of our faith to the public square—not just in each election cycle—but all the time. We will view every issue through a biblical lens, and we won’t become complacent in the next two years. Our mission at My Faith Votes doesn’t change just because the elections are over. We will continue to **PRAY** , **THINK** _biblically_ , and **ACT** _on our faith_. We will stay engaged in the public square in election years and in “off-election” years. We will hold our new leaders accountable as they represent us, and we will continue to stand boldly for the sanctity of life, religious liberty, and marriage and family. Together, we will remain vigilant, informed, and active to be salt and light in our communities, our state, and our country. This is our mission, and what we are called to do, not just every two years, but all the time. Let us continue to do good. Ronald Reagan said it best: “Without God, there is no virtue, because there's no prompting of the conscience. Without God, we're mired in the material, that flat world that tells us only what the senses perceive. Without God, there is a coarsening of the society. And without God, democracy will not and cannot long endure. If we ever forget that we're one nation under God, then we will be a nation gone under.” As we move on from these 2018 midterm elections, join me in praying and standing as one of God’s lights on this, our “shining city on a hill.”
---

name: Ben Jealous
subject: Thank you, truly
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
This is not the end for the issues we believe in.

| Friend, The incredible support you have all given me in this uphill fight, with all the odds stacked against us, means a lot. I want to thank everyone who has been a part of our coalition on behalf of the entire Jealous-Turnbull team. Tuesday night, I called Governor Hogan to congratulate him on his re-election and to wish him the very best in leading our state for the next four years. While we have different visions and values, I have no doubt that he cares deeply about our state and the families who call it home. As General Colin Powell told me a long time ago, "It's easy to figure out what we don't agree on. It's much harder to find out what we do agree on and get it done." I pledge to Governor Hogan that as we find points of agreement, I will work with him to get things done for the people of our state. I also pledge to each of you that the movement we've built, bringing our working families together, will continue on. It was not long ago that many of us were working together to win big victories on civil rights in Maryland. There's no reason why we can't get back to getting big things done on the economy, and healthcare, and education, too. Even as I concede this race, and congratulate Governor Hogan on his victory, I assure everyone who gave their heart, their sweat, and their hard-earned dollars to this campaign, that we will continue to work together. We will continue to make sure the future all our families yearn for comes faster. It has been a great honor to be Maryland's Democratic nominee for governor. At this moment in history, with everything that is going on in the White House and in Congress, I am grateful that this campaign could lend a voice to anyone marginalized by those in power. Even as our state and nation face some of our toughest moments, you have given me the strength to see a more hopeful future. Thank you from the bottom of my heart. Ben
---

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
---

name: Tom O'Halleran
subject: Thank you, friends!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
Last night, voters in Arizona's First District entrusted me with a second term serving them in Congress. I am humbled and grateful for the outpouring of support my campaign has received from Democrats
Tom O'Halleran is running for Congress in Arizona's 1st district, a critical race to getting Congress back on track. Click here to donate to Tom's campaign and help him put Arizona families first.
[](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d633db5/1a8421e1/19b87d12e/4971f346/2401441813/VEsH/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ohalleranq32018?refcode=em_header&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000")
---
Friends, _Thank you._ **Last night, voters in Arizona 's First District entrusted me with a second term serving them in Congress.** I am humbled and grateful for the outpouring of support my campaign has received from Democrats, Republicans, and Independents alike. And I am especially grateful for you -- our grassroots supporters. Our hardworking volunteers spent thousands of hours getting voters to the polls and sharing our message of common-sense problem-solving, and this victory belongs to you. Now more than ever, we need leaders in Washington who will put party politics aside and work together to address the challenges our families and communities face. And last night, the voters of Arizona's First District were clear that hyper-partisan rhetoric and gridlock have no place in our government. In the next two years, I will continue to work with my colleagues on both sides of the aisle to get results for families in the First District and across America. _Thank you again._ I promise I will never stop fighting for you. Yours, Tom
**_**Representative Tom O 'Halleran represents Arizona's First Congressional District in the 115th Congress. As a former police officer, small businessman, and Arizona state legislator, Tom knows what it takes to bring people together and bring positive change to Arizona families. subject: President Trump Calls Mass Shooting at Club in Thousand Oaks, California, ‘Terrible,’ Says He's Been Briefed
body:
“President Donald Trump responded to a mass shooting at a Southern California music club... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=412446427e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# President Trump Calls Mass Shooting at Club in Thousand Oaks, California, ‘Terrible,’ Says He's Been Briefed

“President Donald Trump responded to a mass shooting at a Southern California music club, calling it ‘terrible’ and saying he has been ‘fully briefed’ on the incident,” Cheyenne Haslett reports for ABC News. The President commended the “great bravery shown by police,” thanking law enforcement and asking that God bless all of the victims and their families following this horrible incident.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6244d1fd65&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Brett Kavanaugh formally takes his seat as the 114th justice at the traditional investiture ceremony at the Supreme Court Thursday morning,” Nina Totenberg reports for [NPR](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=65e061a6bc&e=3ee4985eea). “President Trump and first lady Melania Trump are scheduled to attend the ceremony.” On October 6, Justice Kavanaugh was confirmed by the U.S. Senate, and he began hearing arguments on October 9.
---
|
---
| In _[The Washington Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1ceb7e283f&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Ed Rogers writes that by most measures, Republicans beat the odds of history and nearly everyone’s expectations for the 2018 midterms. “The instant analysis is clear: Democrats may have won the House, but Trump won the election.” President Trump and his allies “have an appeal that the elites in New York and Hollywood cannot dismiss or combat.”
---
|
---
| “Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen said Wednesday that this week's midterm elections were the ‘most secure’ the U.S. has ever seen,” Anna Giaritelli writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f0062cfe1e&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Nielsen's department took the lead on election security in early 2017. That job included helping all 10,000 state and local jurisdictions prepare for Election Day.” Giaritelli adds that “DHS has not reported any mass fraudulent voting, amended vote counts, or abnormal disruptions in the nationwide vote.”
---
|

name: Paul Davis
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paul Davis for Kansas
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/7F3th9-m9jV-fClHk1sjhy1AL_zU7pldn23p44pgp6c=/Zz2A-teh2nsNsWt5bc6BkuWiMkkyFHRKFVP63TId4CY=)

Peter,

The 2018 Election has come and gone. While I am disappointed with the outcome of our race, my heart is filled with gratitude to the hundreds - if not thousands - of you that welcomed me into your homes, told me your stories and joined this cause.

You gave of your time, your money, your shoe leather, and so much more. I keep thinking about Kansans like the 89-year-old man who knocked on a thousand doors, the volunteers who canvassed with me in the rain, the 10-year-old who came all the way from California with her mom to support our campaign and elected to spend her birthday working to help this effort. I will never forget you.

There are still many reasons to feel hopeful and to stay just as engaged as you have been throughout this campaign. You joined this team because you love Kansas, and Kansas' new Governor-elect Laura Kelly will need your help in the coming months as she guides our state out of the disastrous Brownback era for good. The actual business of change is not glamorous. It happens one front porch, one phone call, one meet-and-greet at a time. It can be tedious and frustrating. It's slow. It's messy. It's really, really hard work! But that work must continue.

This campaign for public office will be my last, but I'll never stop working to protect and advance our shared Kansas values. You must not either. My Dad, who we lost earlier this year, taught me that government can - and should - be an instrument for good. He taught me that service is an essential ingredient of our democracy. And he taught me that we are all on this journey of life together. I know I will find another way to contribute and do my part.

A few nights ago, as our team drove back to Lawrence after a long day of campaigning, I looked out the car window and caught one of those beautiful Kansas sunsets. It reminded me of how fortunate I feel to live here. This state and her people have been so good to me and my family. There is something special about living in the heartland of America. We look out for our neighbors, we care for those in need and we just love this place because we know...there really is no place like home.

Thank you and God bless you,

Paul
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/7F3th9-m9jV-fClHk1sjhy1AL_zU7pldn23p44pgp6c=/XagJtWi6fLIE5VDlb_hNFcRY0WLVnxHr0VIK32oLdXg=)

Paid for by Paul Davis for Kansas
---

Lawrence KS 66044 United States

name: Joe Manchin
subject: Thank you!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Manchin for West Virginia
body:
Together we made history. Nobody in the United States has ever won in a state that the president, in a previous election, won by 42 points. |
---
| From the bottom of my heart, thank you!

Together we made history. Nobody in the United States has ever won in a state that the president, in a previous election, won by 42 points.

This win would not be possible without the support of friends like you.

I'm tremendously grateful to the people of West Virginia, everyone who donated to our campaign, volunteered their time, and stood with me when it mattered most.

I never expected President Trump to come to this state as much as he did, but you stood tall. What West Virginia said loud and clear on Election Day: "Mr. President, we want our senator, not your senator."

Tuesday's results show that the people of West Virginia want our president to be the president of the United States, not the divided states.

I am deeply honored to have the opportunity to continue serving our great state in the U.S. Senate. It's time to start working together because for me it's all about West Virginia.

Thanks,
Joe

---
| Paid for by Manchin for West Virginia
---
Joe is running for reelection to the United States Senate because West Virginia's working families are still fighting to make a decent living, despite working their hearts out everyday. Joe's not a huge fan of Washington, but the time that he's spent there has been filled with bipartisan bridge-building to try and find commonsense solutions to our shared problems.

name: John Zody
subject: 2019 Begins Now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party
body:
| | | |
---|---
| | Hoosier Democrats – We’re fresh off Election Day, and I just wanted to share a little bit with you as we look ahead. We are saddened that we were not successful in the U.S. Senate race, and we are extremely grateful for Joe Donnelly’s leadership and dedication to our state over the past six years. He served us well by doing things like protecting access to affordable health care – something every one of us agrees is critical. In 2018, the Indiana Democratic Party had more first-time, millennial and female candidates running than in recent memory. And, if our numbers hold, I am pleased to tell you that eight of those candidates will be serving in the next session of the Indiana General Assembly. We are elated that JD Ford won his race for the State Senate, beating an incumbent and reaping the benefits of his years of hard work across District 29. On the House side, we are moving in a positive direction, having gained two seats and with votes still being counted in two more. There are also bright spots in how many of our new candidates performed. While some didn’t win, they out-performed base numbers or previous election results in their districts by considerable amounts. We should be encouraged by this. There's a realignment happening in Indiana and our candidates are on the front lines. So where do we go from here? We look at these gains, these areas of growth – and continue to build, and that starts with our municipal races across the state in 2019. Even in counties where we suffered our greatest losses, there are many opportunities for Democrats to run for city and town council, Clerk and Clerk-Treasurer, and Mayor. In 2015, Democrats performed well in municipal races in Indiana, and we can do it again if we maintain our focus and continue talking to voters. We will have our first municipal candidate training on January 12 – stay tuned for more information! Thank you for everything you did during this election cycle. Our greatest success in 2018 was channeling the new energy in our Party into action. You volunteered. You ran for office. Everyone worked. We continued building the bench in 2018, and we’re going to accelerate that in 2019. Our long-term success will not happen overnight, but the enthusiasm and new and energized candidates are there. John Zody Chair |

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: we see the finish line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
Across our state, election workers are opening up the dreams of Georgians in absentee ballots, and we believe our chance for a stronger Georgia is just within reach. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa7d8/34a064cc/2601421/70cc926/3299865191/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friend, Across our state, election workers are opening up the dreams of Georgians in absentee ballots, and we believe our chance for a stronger Georgia is just within reach. But we cannot seize it until all voices are heard. And I promise you right now that we will make sure that every vote is counted. _Every single vote_. The machinery of democracy should work for everyone, everywhere. Not just for certain people on certain days. What lies on the other side of our efforts? Fully funded public education. Medicaid expansion. And raising family incomes without raising taxes. I believe that we can get there. There are enough uncounted ballots out there to push us into runoff election territory, and I know that in December, we can win _again_. But I need your help: **[We're employing a fully staffed team to ensure that this process is fair for all involved. Chip in $3 to support their work and help us make sure every vote is counted.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa7d8/34a064cc/2601421/70cc927/3299865191/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTA4MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11082018-full")** And if you voted using a provisional ballot, or had any trouble voting on Tuesday, I need you to call our **Voter Protection Hotline at 1-888-730-5816** to let our lawyers know. They can confirm that your provisional ballot is in the system and if not, offer help on what to do next. Our opponent is working hard to take our voices away. To scare us away. But our vision is clear and we see the finish line. And beyond it lies a better, stronger Georgia. Thank you for keeping up this fight with me. Yours, Stacey Abrams

name: Molly Kelly
subject: thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Supporters of Molly Kelly
body:
[](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc4afa/d0ce7de/50856f43/9cdfff9/2191399736/VEsH/ "http://www.mollykelly.com")
[](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc4afa/d0ce7de/50856f43/9cdfff9/2191399736/VEsE/ "http://www.mollykelly.com")
With Election Day behind us, I wanted to take a moment to thank you for all of your support over the last several months.
I ran for Governor because I was concerned that the policies of the Trump Administration had made their way to New Hampshire. Our campaign gave voice to hundreds of thousands of Granite Staters who share that concern.
We took on a tough fight and came close. You stood with me. We worked hard, all across our state. Together, we ran a strong campaign. And we can all be proud of that.
Over the past two months, Governor Sununu and I debated the issues and the challenges facing New Hampshire. Now we must come together to find solutions.
A lot of work lies ahead to make sure New Hampshire works for everyone, and not just a few. Even though we came up short, we can still make a difference, especially with majorities on the Executive Council and in the Senate and House. We can still make New Hampshire the state we believe it should be - one that treats its residents fairly and with dignity and respect.
To all of you who volunteered, who contributed and who believed in this campaign - and fought until the very end - thank you! I will always be grateful for everything you did not just to support me, but to support a better New Hampshire for everyone across our state.
Sincerely,
Molly Kelly
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

body:
This morning, President Donald J. Trump signed a Proclamation as America mourns alongside the victims’ families and communities. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b5d5749790&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • November 8, 2018
---
| | |
# America’s flag flies at half-staff tonight

**Late last night,** 12 people were killed in a horrific shooting in Thousand Oaks, California. This morning, President Donald J. Trump signed a Proclamation as America mourns alongside the victims’ families and communities.

**“As a mark of solemn respect** for the victims of the terrible act of violence,” President Trump writes, “I hereby order that the flag of the United States shall be flown at half-staff at the White House and upon all public buildings and grounds at all military posts and naval stations, and on all naval vessels of the Federal Government in the District of Columbia and throughout the United States and its Territories and possessions until sunset, November 10, 2018.”

**President Trump and Vice President Mike Pence** were briefed on the shooting and the police response early this morning. “Great bravery shown by police. California Highway Patrol was on scene within 3 minutes, with first officer to enter shot numerous times. That Sheriff’s Sergeant died in the hospital,” the President [tweeted](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=56d97e4aa0&e=3ee4985eea).

“**God bless all of the victims** and families of the victims. Thank you to Law Enforcement.”

[Read the President’s Proclamation remembering the victims in Thousand Oaks](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a27aa5d4e0&e=3ee4985eea).

**Acts of courage** : [Sheriff's Sgt. Ron Helus “died a hero.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6f2c1b1080&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Our 114th Supreme Court Justice of the United States

**President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump** joined the 114th Justice of the United States, Brett M. Kavanaugh, for his traditional investiture ceremony at the U.S. Supreme Court this morning.

**The investiture ceremony** marks when a new Justice formally takes his or her spot on the High Court. The President and First Lady joined Justice Kavanaugh, seven of the other justices, and numerous public officials for the occasion. Chief Justice John Roberts administered the judicial oath to Justice Kavanaugh just after 10 a.m.

**Justice Kavanaugh was confirmed** by the U.S. Senate on October 6, and began hearing oral arguments a few days later on October 9.

[See photos from today’s investiture ceremony.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fc2c52749b&e=3ee4985eea)

**More** : [Get to know America’s 114th Supreme Court Justice, Brett M. Kavanaugh](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e1eec83af6&e=3ee4985eea).

#

Friends!

Thank you! We won. And we won big. I got the highest vote total of all House races at 159,666 votes and almost 70% of the vote compared to my opponent at 29%.

You were key to my success. Many of you throughout the state of Arizona have contributed to my campaign and I am grateful for your support.

Your vote for me is a vote to protect our liberty and the Constitution. I am humbled by the confidence you have in me and will continue to stand with President Trump to move his agenda forward. President Trump has stood strong for America. Now it’s time we protect him.

Thank you for standing with me. It’s an honor to work for you.
